title: taraxacum oreinum
intro: Taraxacum oreinum, also known as the common dandelion, is a perennial plant belonging to the sunflower family. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum oreinum lie in its roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain compounds like inulin, taraxasterol, and sesquiterpene lactones, making it a valued herb in traditional medicine. The plant's health benefits include its ability to stimulate digestion, lower blood pressure, and act as a diuretic, as well as its potential to help manage diabetes, reduce inflammation, and support liver health. Taraxacum oreinum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, or consumed as a capsule or powder, with some herbal preparations also using the plant's roots and leaves in combination with other herbs.
